title: Quesnelia vanhoutteana
taxonomy: Quesnelia vanhoutteana is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil. Quesnelia vanhoutteana is a type of epiphytic bromeliad, characterized by its rosette of leaves and funnel-shaped inflorescence. The plant has been classified based on its distinct characteristics and genetic analysis. Quesnelia vanhoutteana is considered an important part of the Bromeliaceae family.

intro: Quesnelia vanhoutteana, also known as the torch plant, is a type of succulent plant native to Brazil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Quesnelia vanhoutteana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
